chain.thought: Explanation: Top-10 finishes occurred in years 2001 (4th), 2002 (2nd), 2005 (2nd/6th), 2006 (2nd/1st/7th), and 2007 (1st). The consecutive-year streaks are 2001–2002 (2 years) and 2005–2007 (3 years). The longest consecutive run is 3 years.

judge_thought: Explanation: The table lists selected competition results by year, with top-10 positions in 2001 (4th), 2002 (2nd), 2005 (2nd/6th), 2006 (1st/7th), and 2007 (1st), and a DQ in 2011. Because the dataset is not continuous across years (e.g., no entries for 2003–2004) and only includes specific events rather than every year of competition, the concept of “consecutive years” cannot be definitively computed from the provided data. In this context, returning “null” appropriately reflects that the number of consecutive years with top-10 finishes is indeterminable from the table. This aligns with the data’s gaps and the DQ in 2011, supporting the Prediction Answer.
